members.name: Format
members.signature: public String Format(int indentAll, int indentFrames)
members.summary: Converts the backtrace to a [String], where the entire string will be indented by [param indent_all] number of spaces, and the individual stack frames will be additionally indented by [param indent_frames] number of spaces. [b]Note:[/b] Calling [method Object.to_string] on a [ScriptBacktrace] will produce the same output as calling [method format] with all parameters left at their default values.

members.name: GetFrameCount
members.summary: Returns the number of stack frames in the backtrace.

members.name: GetFrameFile
members.summary: Returns the file name of the call site represented by the stack frame at the specified index.

members.name: GetFrameFunction
members.summary: Returns the name of the function called at the stack frame at the specified index.

members.name: GetFrameLine
members.summary: Returns the line number of the call site represented by the stack frame at the specified index.

members.name: GetGlobalVariableCount
members.summary: Returns the number of global variables (e.g. autoload singletons) in the backtrace. [b]Note:[/b] This will be non-zero only if the [code]include_variables[/code] parameter was [code]true[/code] when capturing the backtrace with [method Engine.capture_script_backtraces].

members.name: GetGlobalVariableName
members.summary: Returns the name of the global variable at the specified index.

members.name: GetGlobalVariableValue
members.summary: Returns the value of the global variable at the specified index. [b]Warning:[/b] With GDScript backtraces, the returned [Variant] will be the variable's actual value, including any object references. This means that storing the returned [Variant] will prevent any such object from being deallocated, so it's generally recommended not to do so.

members.name: GetLanguageName
members.summary: Returns the name of the script language that this backtrace was captured from.

members.name: GetLocalVariableCount
members.summary: Returns the number of local variables in the stack frame at the specified index. [b]Note:[/b] This will be non-zero only if the [code]include_variables[/code] parameter was [code]true[/code] when capturing the backtrace with [method Engine.capture_script_backtraces].

members.name: GetLocalVariableName
members.signature: public String GetLocalVariableName(int frameIndex, int variableIndex)
members.summary: Returns the name of the local variable at the specified [param variable_index] in the stack frame at the specified [param frame_index].

members.name: GetLocalVariableValue
members.signature: public Variant GetLocalVariableValue(int frameIndex, int variableIndex)
members.summary: Returns the value of the local variable at the specified [param variable_index] in the stack frame at the specified [param frame_index]. [b]Warning:[/b] With GDScript backtraces, the returned [Variant] will be the variable's actual value, including any object references. This means that storing the returned [Variant] will prevent any such object from being deallocated, so it's generally recommended not to do so.

members.name: GetMemberVariableCount
members.summary: Returns the number of member variables in the stack frame at the specified index. [b]Note:[/b] This will be non-zero only if the [code]include_variables[/code] parameter was [code]true[/code] when capturing the backtrace with [method Engine.capture_script_backtraces].

members.name: GetMemberVariableName
members.signature: public String GetMemberVariableName(int frameIndex, int variableIndex)
members.summary: Returns the name of the member variable at the specified [param variable_index] in the stack frame at the specified [param frame_index].

members.name: GetMemberVariableValue
members.signature: public Variant GetMemberVariableValue(int frameIndex, int variableIndex)
members.summary: Returns the value of the member variable at the specified [param variable_index] in the stack frame at the specified [param frame_index]. [b]Warning:[/b] With GDScript backtraces, the returned [Variant] will be the variable's actual value, including any object references. This means that storing the returned [Variant] will prevent any such object from being deallocated, so it's generally recommended not to do so.

members.name: IsEmpty
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the backtrace has no stack frames.
